root_text.iti19:2.1: “Ekadhammo, bhikkhave, loke uppajjamāno uppajjati bahujanahitāya bahujanasukhāya bahuno janassa atthāya hitāya sukhāya devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.iti19:2.4: Saṅghe kho pana, bhikkhave, samagge na ceva aññamaññaṁ bhaṇḍanāni honti, na ca aññamaññaṁ paribhāsā honti, na ca aññamaññaṁ parikkhepā honti, na ca aññamaññaṁ pariccajanā honti.
root_text.iti19:2.5: Tattha appasannā ceva pasīdanti, pasannānañca bhiyyobhāvo hotī”ti.
root_text.iti19:4.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti19:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti19:2.1: “One thing, mendicants, arises in the world for the welfare and happiness of the people, for the people, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of gods and humans.
translation_text.iti19:2.4: When the Saṅgha is in harmony, they don’t argue, insult, block, or forsake each other.
translation_text.iti19:2.5: This inspires confidence in those without it, and increases confidence in those who have it.”
translation_text.iti19:3.2: as is support for those in harmony.
translation_text.iti19:3.3: Taking a stand on the teaching,
translation_text.iti19:3.4: favoring harmony, they ruin no sanctuary.
translation_text.iti19:3.5: After creating harmony in the Saṅgha,
translation_text.iti19:3.6: they rejoice in heaven for an eon.”
translation_text.iti19:4.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
